title: Lab Safety Concern
transcript: Student: Professor Lee, could I talk to you about the chemistry lab? I have some safety concerns. Professor: Certainly. What's on your mind? Student: During last week's experiment, the ventilation fan wasn't working properly, and several students noticed a strong chemical odor. Professor: That's concerning. Did anyone report it to the lab technician? Student: I mentioned it to the TA, but I'm not sure if it was logged. Professor: I'll check the maintenance log immediately. In the meantime, if the fan isn't working during your next session, stop the experiment and evacuate the lab. Student: Should we reschedule the lab if that happens? Professor: Yes. Safety comes first. I'll also send an email to all students reminding them to report equipment issues right away. Student: Thank you. I just want to make sure everyone stays safe. Professor: I appreciate you bringing this to my attention. Please let me know if you notice anything else.

questions.stem: Why does the student speak to Professor Lee?
options: To ask for an extension on a lab report
To report a safety issue in the chemistry lab
To request a different lab section
To discuss a poor grade on an experiment
questions.explanation: The student wants to report that the ventilation fan wasn't working and there was a strong chemical odor during lab.

questions.stem: What does the professor tell the students to do if the fan is not working?
options: Stop the experiment and leave the lab
Open the windows and finish quickly
Report it after the lab is over
questions.explanation: The professor instructs students to stop the experiment and evacuate the lab if the ventilation fan isn't working.

audioSegments.text: Professor Lee, could I talk to you about the chemistry lab? I have some safety concerns.
During last week's experiment, the ventilation fan wasn't working properly, and several students noticed a strong chemical odor.
That's concerning. Did anyone report it to the lab technician?
I mentioned it to the TA, but I'm not sure if it was logged.
I'll check the maintenance log immediately. In the meantime, if the fan isn't working during your next session, stop the experiment and evacuate the lab.
Should we reschedule the lab if that happens?
Yes. Safety comes first. I'll also send an email to all students reminding them to report equipment issues right away.
Thank you. I just want to make sure everyone stays safe.
I appreciate you bringing this to my attention. Please let me know if you notice anything else.
